prompt.question: What is the most common role that has access to almost all platform features, functions, and data?
feedbacks: Super User [sn_super_user] is a role in ServiceNow that provides the highest level of access and control over the platform. While it has extensive privileges, it is not the most common role that has access to almost all platform features, functions, and data.

Security Admin [security_admin] is a role in ServiceNow that focuses on managing security-related configurations and policies within the platform. While important for ensuring data protection and compliance, it does not typically have access to all platform features, functions, and data.

System Administrator [admin] is the most common role in ServiceNow that has access to almost all platform features, functions, and data. This role is responsible for managing the overall system configuration, user access, and data within the platform.

Base Admin [base_admin] is a role in ServiceNow that provides administrative access to the base system tables and configurations. While it has significant privileges, it does not typically have access to all platform features, functions, and data.

System Manager [sys_manager] is a role in ServiceNow that focuses on managing system configurations and integrations. While it has broad access to system settings, it may not have access to all platform features, functions, and data like the System Administrator role does.

question_plain: What is the most common role that has access to almost all platform features, functions, and data?

prompt.question: When moving multiple update sets at one time, what might you do to facilitate the move?
feedbacks: Previewing the update sets before moving them allows you to review the changes that will be applied and ensure that everything is correct. This step helps in identifying any potential issues or conflicts that may arise during the move process.

Using the batch option to move multiple update sets at once can streamline the process and save time. It allows you to group related update sets together and move them as a single unit, reducing the risk of errors and ensuring consistency in the changes being applied.

Listing the update sets provides a visual representation of the sets that are available for moving. While it can be helpful for organization purposes, it does not directly facilitate the move process or make it more efficient.

Mapping update sets may involve creating a visual representation of the dependencies between different sets. While this can be useful for understanding the relationships between sets, it is not a direct action that facilitates the move of multiple update sets at once.

question_plain: When moving multiple update sets at one time, what might you do to facilitate the move?

prompt.question: What do you click when you have made modifications to your report, and you want to see the results without saving?
feedbacks: Preview allows you to see a preview of your report before saving any modifications. It is useful for reviewing changes without committing to them permanently.
Test is not the correct option for viewing modifications without saving in ServiceNow reports. It is typically used for testing functionalities or scenarios within the platform.

Run is the correct choice when you want to see the results of your modifications without saving them permanently. It allows you to execute the report and view the output without making any permanent changes.
Try It is not the appropriate option for viewing modifications without saving in ServiceNow reports. It is usually used for testing specific features or functionalities within the platform.
Execute is not the correct choice for viewing modifications without saving in ServiceNow reports. It is typically used for running specific actions or processes within the platform.

question_plain: What do you click when you have made modifications to your report, and you want to see the results without saving?

prompt.question: Which framework can automatically populate values for the Priority and Category fields based on the Short description field value?
feedbacks: Predictive Intelligence is the correct framework that can automatically populate values for the Priority and Category fields based on the Short description field value. It uses machine learning algorithms to predict and suggest values for these fields, improving efficiency and accuracy in categorizing and prioritizing incidents or requests.

Assignment Rule is not the correct framework for automatically populating values for the Priority and Category fields based on the Short description field value. Assignment Rules are used to assign tasks or incidents to specific groups or individuals based on predefined criteria, not for populating field values.

CSDM (Common Service Data Model) is not the correct framework for automatically populating values for the Priority and Category fields based on the Short description field value. CSDM is a data model that defines common data structures and relationships in ServiceNow, but it does not specifically handle field value population based on other field values.

Action is not the correct framework for automatically populating values for the Priority and Category fields based on the Short description field value. Actions in ServiceNow are used to automate specific tasks or processes, but they do not focus on dynamically populating field values based on other field values.

UI Policy is not the correct framework for automatically populating values for the Priority and Category fields based on the Short description field value. UI Policies are used to dynamically change the behavior or visibility of form elements based on specified conditions, but they do not handle automatic field value population based on other field values.

question_plain: Which framework can automatically populate values for the Priority and Category fields based on the Short description field value?

prompt.question: Which testing framework is used to test ServiceNow Applications?
feedbacks: Test Driven Framework (TDF) is not the testing framework used to test ServiceNow Applications. TDF is a development approach where tests are written before the code, ensuring that the code meets the specified requirements.

Junit is a popular testing framework for Java applications, but it is not specifically used to test ServiceNow Applications. ServiceNow has its own testing framework tailored for testing its applications.

Selenium is a widely used tool for automated testing of web applications, including ServiceNow applications. However, it is not the dedicated testing framework provided by ServiceNow for testing its applications.

Automated Test Framework (ATF) is the testing framework provided by ServiceNow specifically designed for testing ServiceNow Applications. It allows users to create and run automated tests to ensure the functionality and quality of ServiceNow applications.

question_plain: Which testing framework is used to test ServiceNow Applications?

prompt.question: Which allows the creation of a task-based record from Service Catalog?
feedbacks: Assignment Rule is not directly related to creating task-based records from the Service Catalog. It is used to automatically assign tasks or incidents to specific users or groups based on predefined criteria, but it does not create records in the Service Catalog.

Flow Designer is a tool used to automate processes and workflows within ServiceNow, but it is not specifically designed for creating task-based records in the Service Catalog. It focuses more on workflow automation and integration between different applications.

UI Builder is used to create custom user interfaces and applications within ServiceNow, but it is not the tool used for creating task-based records from the Service Catalog. UI Builder focuses on designing user interfaces and experiences, not on record creation.

Record Producers are specifically designed in ServiceNow to allow the creation of task-based records from the Service Catalog. They enable users to submit requests for services or items through the Service Catalog, which then generates a corresponding record in the system for fulfillment.

question_plain: Which allows the creation of a task-based record from Service Catalog?

prompt.question: What module do you use to access the reports that are available to you?
feedbacks: Self-Service > My Reports is not the correct module to access the reports available to you. This module is typically used for accessing personal reports that you have created or saved for your own use, rather than accessing all available reports.

Self-Service > My Dashboards is not the correct module to access the reports available to you. This module is usually used for accessing personal dashboards that you have created or saved for your own use, rather than accessing all available reports.

Reports > View / Run is the correct module to access the reports that are available to you. This module allows you to view and run reports that have been shared with you or are accessible based on your permissions within the ServiceNow platform.

Reports > Homepage is not the correct module to access the reports available to you. The Homepage module typically provides an overview of various features and functionalities within the ServiceNow platform, but it is not specifically designed for accessing reports.

Reports > Overview is not the correct module to access the reports available to you. The Overview module may provide a summary or high-level view of reports, but it is not the primary module for accessing and running reports within the ServiceNow platform.

question_plain: What module do you use to access the reports that are available to you?

prompt.question: Security rules are defined to restrict the permissions of users from viewing and interacting with data. What are these security rules called?
feedbacks: CRUD Rules typically refer to Create, Read, Update, and Delete rules that define the basic operations that can be performed on data. While these rules are related to data manipulation, they are not specifically focused on restricting user permissions like security rules do.

Access Control Rules are the correct choice as they are specifically designed to restrict the permissions of users from viewing and interacting with data. These rules define who can access what data and what actions they can perform, ensuring data security and compliance within the system.

Role Assignment Rules are used to assign roles to users based on their responsibilities and access needs within the system. While roles play a crucial part in defining user permissions, they are not the same as security rules that directly control data access restrictions.

Scripted User Rules are not a standard term in the context of ServiceNow security rules. While scripts can be used to enforce security measures, they are typically part of Access Control Rules or other security configurations rather than being a separate type of rule.

User Authentication Rules are primarily focused on verifying the identity of users during the login process. These rules ensure that users are who they claim to be before granting them access to the system, but they do not directly control the permissions or restrictions on data access like security rules do.

question_plain: Security rules are defined to restrict the permissions of users from viewing and interacting with data. What are these security rules called?
